Incinerator ash was landfilled, as all incinerator ash considered
nonhazardous per dioxin and furans testing. HCB not tested for in landfill ash.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

HCB was not tested for in the incinerator bottom ash. All incinerator ash considered
nonhazardous per dioxin and furans testing, and was landfilled.

comments.comment: Incineration emissions, and incinerator bottom ash.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: All camp incinerator D&F emissions are below the allowable decimal place entry (<0.000001 g TEQ) in the SWIM data fields. Based on stack and ash testing. However, SWIM would not let progress in the data entry unless a value above the decimal accuracy (0.000001) is entered for D&F. Estimated incinerator air emission g TEQ D&F total is 0.00000001, and landfilled ash tested as clean.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: All camp incinerator D&F emissions are below the allowable decimal place entry (<0.000001 g TEQ) in the SWIM data fields. Based on stack and ash testing. However, SWIM would not let progress in the data entry unless a value above the decimal accuracy (0.000001) is entered for D&F. Estimated incinerator. Landfilled ash tested as clean.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Stack tests performed on the incinerators in 2023 for individual cogenors, but all emission estimate values were below the decimal place limitations of the data entry into SWIM. Entered the calculated total D&F instead. Last year's value (0.000001) had been dummied in due to restrictions with the SWIM decimal limitations. This year's value allowed more accuracy for total D&F.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: SWIM would not allow entry of the small emissions stack release value (0.0000001 gTEQ) as it is below 6 decimal places. Entered zero for total D/F instead. Note that for LoQ, one stack tested was above 32 pg/m3 where the other was below. Entered as "above LoQ".

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: No hazardous ash disposed offsite previous year. Small amount of ash in 2024 were sent for offsite disposal (5 drums). For bottom ash disposal used the UNEP toolkit value of 5 ng TEQ/kg bottom ash for 2024 ash disposal estimate. This may be a conservative value - onsite landfilled ash was considered "clean" per leachate testing.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: Incinerator stack releases and bottom ash

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Increase in stationary combustion source fuel consumption - Note: no speciated VOCs above 1 t

reasonsForPlan.en: Plan was required by notice under the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999
reasonsForPlan.fr: Plan exigé par un avis de planification de la prévention de la pollution publié en vertu de la Loi canadienne sur la protection de l'environnement, 1999

reasonsForPlan.en: Plan was prepared or implemented for another government jurisdiction or Parliament (i.e. provincial government)
reasonsForPlan.fr: Plan préparé ou mis en œuvre pour une autre autorité gouvernementale (c.-à-d., autre ministère fédéral, province, municipalité)

p2Plan.planComment: Please refer to the Waste Management Plan, Incinerator Management Plan, and Air Quality and Emissions Management Plan on the Mackenzie Valley Land and Water Board website.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Last year wind data used to estimate wind erosion emissions was based on Yellowknife wind gust data. This year, wind data from the on-site weather station was used instead as it is more applicable. 
The surface roughness height used in the wind erosion emission estimates has also been updated. In 2021, the surface roughness height used was 10 cm, this year it has been updated to 0.5 cm. The updated surface roughness height is more appropriate based on US EPA AP-42 guidance.

type.fr: Raisons pour exclure les quantités dans les résidus miniers ou dans les stériles

therefore excluded from reporting.

comments.comment:
Last year wind data used to estimate wind erosion emissions was based on Yellowknife wind gust data. This year, wind data from the on-site weather station was used instead as it is more applicable. 
The surface roughness height used in the wind erosion emission estimates has also been updated. In 2021, the surface roughness height used was 10 cm, this year it has been updated to 0.5 cm. The updated surface roughness height is more appropriate based on US EPA AP-42 guidance.

type.en: Reasons for excluding quantities in tailings or waste rock
type.fr: Raisons pour exclure les quantités dans les résidus miniers ou dans les stériles

comments.comment:
Waste Rock has sulphur and arsenic values below the trigger threshold,
therefore excluded from reporting.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: All camp incinerator D&F emissions are below the allowable decimal place entry (<0.000001 g TEQ) in the SWIM data fields. Based on stack and ash testing. However, SWIM would not let progress in the data entry unless a value above the decimal accuracy (0.000001) is entered for D&F. Estimated incinerator air emission TEQ D&F total is 0.00000001, and landfilled ash tested as clean.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: All camp incinerator D&F emissions are below the allowable decimal place entry (<0.000001 g TEQ) in the SWIM data fields. Based on stack and ash testing. However, SWIM would not let progress in the data entry unless a value above the decimal accuracy (0.000001) is entered for D&F. Estimated incinerator. Landfilled ash tested as clean.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Last year wind data used to estimate wind erosion emissions was based on Yellowknife wind gust data. This year, wind data from the on-site weather station was used instead as it is more applicable. 
The surface roughness height used in the wind erosion emission estimates has also been updated. In 2021, the surface roughness height used was 10 cm, this year it has been updated to 0.5 cm. The updated surface roughness height is more appropriate based on US EPA AP-42 guidance.

In addition to the wind erosion method updates, road dust emission estimates increase from 2021 due to correcting an error in the 2021 vehicle kilometres traveled (VKT) estimation method.
